mnemonics.title: String Functions Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: STRING FUNCTIONS: COPY IS DONE BY strcpy, LENGTH IS strlen, CAT IS strcat, COMPARE IS strcmp, REVERSE IS strrev, UPPER IS strupr, LOWER IS strlwr - REMEMBER: C.L.C.C.R.U.L

mnemonics.title: strcmp Return Values
mnemonics.content: strcmp RETURNS: ZERO FOR EQUAL, NEGATIVE IF FIRST STRING IS LESS (COMES FIRST ALPHABETICALLY), POSITIVE IF FIRST STRING IS GREATER - REMEMBER: EQUAL IS ZERO, NEGATIVE MEANS 'LEFT' IS SMALLER

mnemonics.title: String Terminator
mnemonics.content: ALWAYS REMEMBER: STRINGS END WITH '\0' - THIS NULL CHARACTER IS INVISIBLE BUT ESSENTIAL - WITHOUT IT, STRING FUNCTIONS CANNOT FIND THE END

acronyms.expansion: American Standard Code for Information Interchange
acronyms.usage: Used for character encoding in C strings, determines order in string comparison
Terminates strings in C, marks the end of character arrays treated as strings
Used in file string operations and fgets return value checking

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A BOX WITH CONSECUTIVE CELLS: ['H']['e']['l']['l']['o']['\0'] - EACH CELL HOLDS ONE CHARACTER, LAST CELL IS ALWAYS NULL TERMINATOR
IMAGINE TWO BOXES: SOURCE BOX CONTAINS "HELLO", DESTINATION BOX IS EMPTY - strcpy COPIES EACH CHARACTER ONE BY ONE FROM SOURCE TO DESTINATION INCLUDING '\0'
IMAGINE TWO STRINGS SIDE BY SIDE: "HELLO" + "WORLD" - strcat FINDS THE END OF FIRST STRING (WHERE '\0' IS) AND COPIES SECOND STRING THERE - RESULT: "HELLOWORLD"
DRAW A TABLE WITH ROWS AS STRINGS: ROW 0: [A][L][I][C][E][\0], ROW 1: [B][O][B][\0], ROW 2: [C][H][A][R][L][I][E][\0] - EACH ROW IS INDEPENDENT STRING

analogies.analogy: LIKE A RED FLAG AT THE END OF A RACE TRACK - IT TELLS RUNNERS (STRING FUNCTIONS) WHERE THE RACE (STRING) ENDS, EVEN THOUGH THE TRACK (MEMORY) CONTINUES BEYOND IT

analogies.concept: Character Array vs String Literal Pointer
analogies.analogy: char arr[] IS LIKE HAVING YOUR OWN PHOTOCOPY OF A DOCUMENT (YOU CAN EDIT IT). char *ptr IS LIKE HAVING THE ORIGINAL DOCUMENT ON LOAN (READ-ONLY, OTHERS MAY MODIFY IT)
COMPARING STRINGS WITH strcmp IS LIKE LOOKING AT TWO BOOKS PAGE BY PAGE - IF ALL PAGES MATCH, THEY ARE EQUAL (RETURNS 0); IF ONE DIFFERS FIRST, THAT ONE IS 'LESSER' OR 'GREATER'
